column4: abnormal play (diablerie, effects putting cards in play) ignores requirements — 1.6's named scope
continue-as-if-unblocked is implemented as a zone move; 1.11 because the ash heap location is the gating condition
prevention cards may be played before there is damage to prevent — prevention window
ordering of discard-phase effects vs. an end-of-turn return, plus loss of control voiding the return; touches the discard phase but the general content is ordering + control
going to torpor as trigger, its interruption, and end of combat consequences
comes back unlocked is not unlocking — the canonical distinction
multiple copies of a periodic effect are cumulative and each is paid for separately
duration of a prohibition: from block attempt to end of action, even on a failed block
unpreventable damage means prevention cards cannot be played at all, not even to cycle
partial applicability when the bearer does not meet the card's trait clause; haven/equipment dual nature
an ally recruited this turn may still lock to use an ability — same template as R1521
burn blood effects stack; 1.7 owns burn-blood-to effects, 1.2 the cumulativity template
temporary control meeting minion burning [calibration answer]
mandatory vs. optional effects
the "ends unsuccessfully immediately" template closes the window for further modifiers/reactions
a re-entering card is a new entity for all purposes, including NRA repetition limits
timing relative to the press step and survival of a premature round end
cannot attempt to block template — blocking, not stealth and not lock/unlock
re-block opportunities after continuation; a prior decline stands
damage in combat vs. damage after combat

restriction on which vampires this specific card may be moved to; the exclusion follows entirely from what the card grants. Not 1.6 (no requirement principle stated) and not 1.13 (no contest, just a prohibition)
which reaction/modifier windows remain open once the "would be successful" point is passed
a card may be played even where it will have no effect; contrast R0195
hand size: cards that are not replaced still count against hand size. General and homeless — 1.9 owns replacement but nothing owns the hand-size count itself. Candidate section: 6.9 hand-and-discard
a dodge may still be declared even when it will accomplish nothing
shared hand-size vocabulary but the general content is contesting [calibration contrast case]
cannot attempt to block template
abnormal entry ignores requirements and cost, and the entrant may act the turn it arrives
an effect keeps track of a minion across a burn and a return to play
an effect lifting the who-may-block restriction does not lift the other block requirements
range changes take effect immediately for cards keyed to range
a stealth penalty applies to every action including the action provided to burn the card itself
prevent up to X may be used for less
a temporary trait override takes precedence over a permanent trait change; note no code cleanly owns sect as a trait
uncontrolled vampires are not legal targets
quantity reading of this card's own search wording; nothing general — 1.1's "reading card text" is about card text vs. rulebook conflicts, not disambiguating one card's own count
cannot attempt to block template — deliberately NOT 5.2 even though the card unlocks
ordering of a card's payout relative to the referendum's own effects
as a vampire allows an ally to call a referendum
minions in torpor still count for a "control a X" check
an effect that employs is not the employ action, so action-keyed effects do not trigger
draw-then-discard effects: the discarded card need not be one just drawn. General and homeless — no code owns drawing/discarding or hand management. Candidate section: 6.9 hand-and-discard
a card named at announcement is fixed then, is not spent if the action fails, and must be in hand
an Imbued's cost is its starting life, and influencing is not recruiting
presses apply to the current round only
special hunt actions and what they may target
failing to block because of stealth still counts as declining to block
the effect reads the final vote disposition at tally; abstain, even forced, is abstain
an effect keyed to the targets of a prior action applies when the new action targets a subset; borderline card-specific
merging is not affected by effects on vampire cards played normally
a stolen minion returns to its controller in whatever state it is in, torpor included
which cards the slave who entered combat in another minion's place may use; pairs with R1599
a granted Black Hand trait depends on the sect prerequisite
an already-locked minion can be locked again to satisfy a lock requirement
attempting to block and playing further reactions are options, not obligations
the retrieved card is named at declaration
an ally recruited by a card effect cannot act the turn it arrives
a window exists to add a counter before a zero-counter burn resolves
being ousted makes minions leave the ready region, which triggers leave-play effects
retainer damage and environmental damage are excluded from "damage inflicted by the minions"
a cost-payment effect keyed to cards cannot pay for cardless actions or actions provided by cards in play
a thwarted diablerie is unsuccessful: no trophy, no blood hunt
requirements do not apply when taking control of a card already in play
the Edge is granted before the after-bleed pool effect, so the pool gain applies
identical to R0247 — recruited ally may lock to use an ability the same turn
a strike card cannot supply the hand strike another strike card calls for
cannot attempt to block template
the trait acquired by the resolving action is in place for effects played after resolution
which cards the substituting slave may use; pairs with R1183
directedness is determined by which Methuselahs control qualifying cards
when several blood gains occur, even from one card, the controller chooses which one an effect modifies
first strike separates the weapon strike from an effect not itself given first strike
mandatory actions must be taken before optional ones; no code owns minion-phase action ordering, 2.4 is the nearest
Aye and Orun are retrieved in equal pairs for a Laibon entering play at influence
cards chosen at declaration are fixed then and the card itself is excluded
pay what you can: the payment is partial, the effect still fails, the vampire ends empty
a canceled card's costs are still paid — the canonical canceled-cost principle
a stolen minion owed to an ousted Methuselah is removed from the game
damage from a card effect outside a strike is environmental
environmental damage is not damage inflicted by the acting vampire
cannot attempt to block template
requirements to play are checked at play time; the card's own effect removes its own requirement
the burning of this card does not count as a minion being burned for burn-triggered effects
a strike may be used even when the resource its effect consumes is absent
the "as played" window applies to out-of-turn master cards too
damage already queued is applied even if combat ends prematurely
mandatory vs. optional effects — same template as R0325
cannot attempt to block template
wakes are the exception that may be played inside the "as played" window; instance of R2593
bleed modifier arithmetic combines to a net value
cancellation reaches only minion cards played from hand in the normal fashion; identical principle to R2599
the retrieved card is named at declaration — same template as R1287
hand-size vocabulary, but the general content is that an effect keyed to a master phase action triggers once per such action; not the hand-size gap
a title suppressed by an event yields no ballots, but the subreferendum and non-title bonus ballots persist
a cost reduction changes the cost other effects read
a title that only enables the action is not seen by other effects during the referendum
when a cost may be paid in blood or pool, the Methuselah playing the card chooses
queuing a new combat loses effects that would fire after the old combat ends, except unlocks
rescue cost reduction applies regardless of who pays it, and is not optional
a minion returning at the start of the unlock phase still unlocks normally
titles and their votes survive a sect change
counter-driven damage is environmental — same principle as R1859
a press-granting ability is usable only in the press step
distinguishes a choice made as the card is played from a choice made by an effect of the card in play; borderline C, kept because the play-time/in-play distinction is general
scope of one vampire's own ability, fixed by her card text; not 1.5, which is about ability use vs. card play, and not 4.1, which is combat structure
locquipment: a location that is equipment can be paid for by an equipment-cost effect
immune means damage is never inflicted, regardless of preventability or of being outside combat
as the action is announced modifiers precede all other modifiers
the "as played" window: no replacement, immediate cancellation, wakes the only exception
cancellation reaches only minion cards played from hand in the normal fashion; identical to R2145
hunt value increases apply and the blood comes from the hunt target
